Generate list of lists that contain indices of bases in an OTI number

Module Contents

degp_utils.flatten_der_indices(indices)[source]
degp_utils.differences_by_dim_func(X1, X2, n_order, index=-1)[source]
degp_utils.nrmse(y_true, y_pred, norm_type='minmax')[source]

Compute the Normalized Root Mean Squared Error (NRMSE).

Parameters:

  • y_true (array-like) – Ground truth values.

  • y_pred (array-like) – Predicted values.

  • norm_type (str) – Normalization type: - ‘minmax’: divide by (max - min) of y_true - ‘mean’: divide by mean of y_true - ‘std’: divide by standard deviation of y_true

Returns:

NRMSE value.

Return type:

float

degp_utils.transform_nested_list(nested_list)[source]

Recursively traverse a nested list. Whenever we encounter a two-element list [k, v] with both k and v as integers, transform k -> 2*k.

degp_utils.convert_index_to_exponent_form(lst)[source]
degp_utils.build_companion_array(nvars, order, der_indices)[source]
degp_utils.compare_OTI_indices(nvars, order, term_check)[source]

Generate list of lists that contain indices of bases in an OTI number

degp_utils.gen_OTI_indices(nvars, order)[source]

Generate list of lists that contain indices of bases in an OTI number

degp_utils.reshape_y_train(y_train)[source]
degp_utils.transform_cov(cov, sigma_y, sigmas_x, der_indices, X_test)[source]
degp_utils.transform_predictions(y_pred, mu_y, sigma_y, sigmas_x, der_indices, X_test)[source]
degp_utils.normalize_x_data_test(X_test, sigmas_x, mus_x)[source]
degp_utils.normalize_x_data_train(X_train)[source]
degp_utils.normalize_y_data(X_train, y_train, der_indices)[source]
degp_utils.rbf_kernel(differences, length_scales, n_order, n_bases, kernel_func, der_indices, powers, index=-1)[source]
ARD RBF kernel for multi-dimensional inputs:

k(x, x’) = sigma_f^2 * exp( -0.5 * sum_{d}((x_d - x’_d)^2 / ell_d^2) ).

Parameters:

  • X1 (array of shape (N, D))

  • X2 (array of shape (M, D))

  • length_scales (array of shape (D,), each dimension's length scale)

  • sigma_f (float (signal amplitude))

Returns:

K

Return type:

array of shape (N, M)
